medicaid.name: Medicaid
medicaid.description: Government health coverage for low-income individuals and families
eligibility_rules: Income must be at or below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL)
Must be a U.S. citizen or qualifying immigrant
Must be a resident of the state where applying
Covers children, pregnant women, adults, elderly, and people with disabilities
In expansion states: adults 19-64 earning up to 138% FPL qualify
In non-expansion states: eligibility may be more restricted for adults without children
medicaid.how_to_apply: Apply through your state Medicaid agency or at healthcare.gov

aca_marketplace.name: ACA Health Insurance Marketplace
aca_marketplace.description: Subsidized private insurance plans for people who do not qualify for Medicaid
eligibility_rules: Must be a U.S. citizen or qualifying immigrant
Must not have access to affordable employer-sponsored insurance
Income between 100% and 400% FPL qualifies for premium tax credits
Open enrollment is November 1 through January 15
Special enrollment periods available for life events

plan_tiers.bronze: Lowest premium, highest out-of-pocket costs. Best if you rarely use healthcare.
plan_tiers.silver: Mid-range premium. Required for Cost Sharing Reductions (CSR). Best for most people.
plan_tiers.gold: Higher premium, lower out-of-pocket. Best if you use healthcare frequently.
plan_tiers.platinum: Highest premium, lowest out-of-pocket. Best for high healthcare users.

aca_marketplace.how_to_apply: Apply at healthcare.gov during open enrollment

chip.name: Children's Health Insurance Program
chip.description: Low-cost coverage for children in families earning too much for Medicaid
chip.federal_poverty_level_threshold: Up to 200% FPL in most states
eligibility_rules: Child must be under 19 years old
Most states cover children up to 200% FPL
Child must be a U.S. citizen or qualifying immigrant
Pregnant women may qualify in some states
chip.how_to_apply: Apply at insurekidsnow.gov or your state CHIP program

medicare.name: Medicare
medicare.description: Federal health insurance for people 65+ and certain younger people with disabilities

parts.part_a: Hospital insurance — covers inpatient hospital stays, skilled nursing facility, hospice. Most people get Part A premium-free.
parts.part_b: Medical insurance — covers doctor visits, outpatient care, preventive services. Standard premium is $174.70/month (2024).
parts.part_c: Medicare Advantage — private plans that bundle Parts A and B, often include Part D.
parts.part_d: Prescription drug coverage — offered through private insurers.

eligibility: Under 65 with certain disabilities after 24 months of Social Security Disability Insurance
Any age with End-Stage Renal Disease or ALS

enrollment_periods.initial: 7-month window starting 3 months before your 65th birthday month

medicare.how_to_apply: Apply at ssa.gov or medicare.gov or call 1-800-MEDICARE

tricare.name: TRICARE
tricare.description: Health coverage for military members, retirees, and their families
plans: TRICARE Prime — HMO-style, lowest cost, requires referrals
TRICARE Select — PPO-style, more flexibility, higher cost
TRICARE for Life — Medicare supplement for military retirees with Medicare
TRICARE Reserve Select — for National Guard and Reserve members
eligibility: Active duty service members and their families
Retired service members and their families
National Guard and Reserve members (some plans)

va_health.name: VA Health Care
va_health.description: Health benefits for Veterans through the Department of Veterans Affairs
eligibility: Veterans who served in active military, naval, or air service
Discharged or released under conditions other than dishonorable
Priority groups 1-8 based on service-connected disabilities, income, and other factors
what_it_covers: Mental health services including PTSD treatment
va_health.how_to_apply: Apply online at va.gov/health-care/apply or call 1-877-222-8387

cobra.name: COBRA Continuation Coverage
cobra.description: Allows you to keep your employer health insurance for a limited time after losing coverage
eligibility_events: Job loss (voluntary or involuntary, except for gross misconduct)
Divorce or legal separation from covered employee
Dependent child aging off parent's plan
cobra.duration: Up to 18 months for job loss or reduced hours. Up to 36 months for other qualifying events.
cobra.cost: You pay the full premium (employee + employer share) plus up to 2% administrative fee. This can be significantly more expensive than when employed.
cobra.deadline: You have 60 days to elect COBRA after losing coverage
cobra.how_to_apply: Contact your employer's HR department or plan administrator

marketplace_special_enrollment.name: Special Enrollment Period (SEP)
marketplace_special_enrollment.description: A window outside open enrollment to sign up for ACA coverage after a qualifying life event
qualifying_events: Loss of health coverage (job loss, aging off parent plan, loss of Medicaid)
Changes in household (marriage, divorce, birth, adoption)
Changes in residence (moving to a new coverage area)
marketplace_special_enrollment.window: Generally 60 days from the qualifying event
marketplace_special_enrollment.how_to_apply: Apply at healthcare.gov and select your qualifying event

community_engagement.name: Community Engagement Requirements
community_engagement.description: Work or activity requirements being added to some state Medicaid programs

new_york_2027.requirement: 80 hours per month of approved activities
exemptions: Physical or mental health condition preventing work
Caring for a child under 14 or disabled family member
Enrolled in TANF or SNAP work programs

federal_poverty_levels_2024.note: Alaska and Hawaii have higher thresholds

options: Add baby to existing insurance within 30-60 days
Apply for Medicaid or CHIP for child
Qualify for Special Enrollment Period on Marketplace
had_baby.deadline: 30-60 days from birth depending on your plan

options: COBRA continuation (up to 18 months)
ACA Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (60 days)
Medicaid if income drops below 138% FPL
lost_job.deadline: 60 days from job loss for SEP, 60 days for COBRA election

options: Add spouse to employer plan within 30 days
Apply together on Marketplace SEP
Check joint Medicaid eligibility
Get own employer coverage if available
ACA Marketplace SEP (60 days)
Medicaid if income qualifies
Enroll in Medicare Part A and B
Consider Medicare Advantage (Part C)
Add Part D for prescriptions
Medicaid for pregnant women (higher income limits)
CHIP for pregnant women in some states
Marketplace plan with maternity coverage
pregnant.note: Many states have expanded Medicaid eligibility specifically for pregnant women

options: Apply for SSDI (Social Security Disability Insurance)
After 24 months on SSDI, automatically qualify for Medicare
SSI recipients automatically get Medicaid in most states
